---
title: "MIMIC-IV ICD-10 Auto-Coding — Evaluation Report"
author: "Nancy Tanaka"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    theme: cosmo
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
---

# Summary

*To be filled after baseline training completes.*

One paragraph: model choice, cohort, headline metrics, delta versus Mullenbach et al. 2018.

# Research Question

Given a free-text discharge summary, predict the set of ICD-10 codes assigned to that admission. Benchmark against Mullenbach et al. 2018 CAML (MIMIC-III top-50 ICD-9).

# Data

Cohort construction, composition, and limitations are documented in [`reports/data_card.md`](data_card.md). Headline numbers:

- MIMIC-IV-Note v2.2 + MIMIC-IV v3.1 Hosp, joined on `hadm_id`.
- Top-50 ICD-10 codes covering 91.04% of admissions; long tail spans 19,440 distinct codes.
- Modelable cohort: **122,288 admissions**.
- Splits: patient-level 80/10/10 by `subject_id`, seed = 42.

# Methods

| Component | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Preprocessing | Collapse `___` to `[REDACTED]`, normalize whitespace, drop notes under 100 tokens | Minimal intervention; preserves clinical language |
| Split | Patient-level, seed = 42 | 44.83% of patients have multiple admissions; admission-level splits would leak |
| Baseline | TF-IDF (1–2 gram, min_df = 5) + one-vs-rest Logistic Regression | Interpretable control; defines the floor every transformer must clear |
| Primary transformer | Bio_ClinicalBERT with chunk-and-max-pool over 512-token chunks | 98.74% of notes exceed a single 512-token window; chunking recovers the signal |
| Fallback | Clinical-Longformer, 4K tokens | Escalation only if chunked BERT misses target by more than 3 F1 points |
| Thresholds | Per-label F1 optimization on validation set | Label prevalence spans ~1–20%; a single global threshold underfits rare labels |

Full rationale in [`DECISIONS.md`](../DECISIONS.md).

# Results

## Headline Metrics (Test Split)

| Metric | Baseline (TF-IDF + LR) | Chunked Bio_ClinicalBERT | Mullenbach 2018 (MIMIC-III) | Target | Floor |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Micro F1 | TBD | TBD | 0.539 | ≥ 0.70 | 0.55 |
| Macro F1 | TBD | TBD | 0.088 | ≥ 0.55 | 0.40 |
| P@5 | TBD | TBD | 0.609 | ≥ 0.70 | — |
| P@8 | TBD | TBD | 0.523 | ≥ 0.65 | — |

## Per-Label Error Analysis

Top 10 lowest-F1 labels with likely error source for each (sub-code fragmentation, low prevalence, documentation style). TBD.

## Calibration

Reliability curve and Brier score per label. TBD.

## Fairness Slices

Macro F1 reported by:

- **Gender** (F vs. M)
- **Age bucket** (18-29, 30-44, 45-54, 55-64, 65-74, 75-84, 85+) with the caveat that `anchor_age` is capped at 89
- **Admission frequency** (single-admit vs. 5+ admits)

Temporal fairness deferred pending Silver-stage date un-shifting. Race- and ethnicity-stratified analysis deferred pending Bronze schema extension. See [`DECISIONS.md`](../DECISIONS.md) 2026-04-21.

# Comparison to Mullenbach 2018

Metric deltas are reported with the following caveats:

- Cohort differences — size (47K MIMIC-III vs. 122K here), era (pre-2015 vs. 2008–2019), code system (ICD-9 vs. ICD-10).
- ICD-10 sub-code fragmentation redistributes common conditions (CHF, CKD, COPD, T2DM) across several codes, so top-50 label spaces are not identical.
- CAML trained on full-length notes with custom attention; this work uses chunked Bio_ClinicalBERT.

Deltas are informative but not strictly apples-to-apples.

# Limitations

See [`reports/data_card.md`](data_card.md). Key points relevant to eval interpretation:

- Cohort is 48% of all ICD-10 admissions in v3.1 Hosp, limited by MIMIC-IV-Note v2.2 sampling.
- Temporal and race-stratified fairness evaluation are deferred, not absent.
- Top-50 K is comparability-driven, not coverage-optimal; raising K would improve coverage but weaken the CAML comparison.

# References

- Mullenbach J, Wiegreffe S, Duke J, Sun J, Eisenstein J. Explainable prediction of medical codes from clinical text. NAACL 2018. <https://arxiv.org/abs/1802.05695>
- Alsentzer E, et al. Publicly available clinical BERT embeddings. Clinical NLP Workshop 2019. <https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.03323>
- Beltagy I, Peters ME, Cohan A. Longformer: The long-document transformer. 2020. <https://arxiv.org/abs/2004.05150>
- Johnson A, et al. MIMIC-IV: A freely accessible electronic health record dataset. Scientific Data 2023.
- Mitchell M, et al. Model Cards for Model Reporting. FAT* 2019.
- Pushkarna M, et al. Data Cards: Purposeful and Transparent Dataset Documentation. FAccT 2022.
